title: pedicularis lutescens var. typica
intro: Pedicularis lutescens var. typica, also known as the golden lousewort, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The golden lousewort has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
